what_it_does: This file registers the corpus-analysis mechanisms for the design system. Each mechanism entry declares what it reads, what it emits, how findings are routed, the tool that implements it, and what a future local-model augmentation adds. It is an extend-by-registration catalog for the Layer-0 structural floor.
observations: It is described as the Layer-0 structural floor: exact, free, deterministic, and model-free.
Three mechanisms are registered: code-ref-validator, code-symbol-registry, and code-dependency-graph.
Every entry carries id, layer, reads, emits, routes_on, tool, and model_augmentation fields.
New mechanisms are added by registering a new id-keyed entry without surgery, mirroring addresses.json and tokens.json.

code-symbol-registry already implements model augmentation X11 using nodes/embed and nodes/retrieve (BGE-M3 at :8001) to produce semantically_nearest[] edges, with degrade-with-warning if the embedder is unreachable.
code-dependency-graph emits a symbol->symbol dependency graph (Convergence X10) with bounded reach queries capped at CODEEDGES_DEPTH (default 2) and fail-loud handling of stale source files.
code-ref-validator checks code: refs in register.json and addresses.json and routes drift findings to corpus repair or the build track.
model_augmentation notes describe future semantic judgement layers that sit on top of the structural outputs.
standouts: The registry explicitly separates the deterministic structural floor from later semantic/model augmentation, reserving a model_augmentation seam in every entry.
code-symbol-registry already realizes X11 semantic nearest-neighbor edges via existing embed/retrieve nodes and degrades gracefully with a warning when :8001 is unreachable.
code-dependency-graph introduces a bounded, fail-loud symbol-level dependency graph using the same code:// coordinate system as the symbol registry.
conventions: Follows an extend-by-registration pattern keyed by mechanism id.
Uses a shared code://<file-stem>/<symbol> coordinate system across symbol and dependency mechanisms.
Separates reads, emits, routes_on, tool, and model_augmentation per entry.
Embeds long prose documentation and project-specific shorthand (X10, X11, X14, Block 17-18) inside JSON string values.
concerns: The model_augmentation and routes_on strings rely on project-specific shorthand that may be opaque without external context.
routes_on values mix routing logic and downstream consumer names in compressed expressions, which may resist programmatic parsing.
The file combines registry schema documentation, operational tool references, and future AI augmentation plans in the same value fields.
notes: This is the extend-by-registration catalog of structural corpus-analysis mechanisms, with each entry also reserving a future local-model augmentation seam.
questions: What is the exact schema or grammar for routes_on strings?
Where are the referenced external files and tools (register.json, addresses.json, _system/refcheck.py, _system/symbols.py, _system/codeedges.py, nodes/embed, nodes/retrieve) located and versioned?
How are mechanism ids discovered and invoked by the build or keeper workflow?
What triggers or schedule is intended for running each mechanism?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual content: it is a registry of corpus-analysis mechanisms with the documented fields and extension pattern.
apparent_themes: Graceful degradation of optional model layers
Single coordinate system for code symbols
intention_signals: It is trying to build a self-describing, extensible pipeline of corpus-analysis tools that produce actionable findings, while keeping the deterministic structural layer cleanly separated from optional local-AI semantic judgement.
novelty: It is distinctive in explicitly reserving a model_augmentation seam in every structural mechanism, and in already wiring one mechanism (code-symbol-registry) to compute semantic nearest-neighbor edges with degrade-with-warning when the embedder is unreachable.
contribution: It contributes a structured, extensible registry of three code-corpus analysis mechanisms, their inputs, outputs, routing rules, tool bindings, and a documented seam for future semantic augmentation, using a shared code:// coordinate system.
summary_for_embedding: company/design/_system/mechanisms.json is an extend-by-registration catalog of three Layer-0 structural corpus-analysis mechanisms that read the design corpus, emit drift, symbol, and dependency findings, route them to repair or build tracks, and document a future local-model augmentation seam for each.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to make the structural analysis pipeline discoverable, extensible, and ready for staged local-AI semantic augmentation without altering the deterministic floor.
